title: ‘Look inwards’: India tears into Pakistan over Karachi blast allegations
content: India has firmly rejected Pakistan's claims linking it to the recent Karachi terrorist attack. The Ministry of External Affairs urged Islamabad to address its internal terror infrastructure and cease using terrorism as a state policy, rather than making baseless accusations. The incident involved militants attacking a paramilitary headquarters, resulting in casualties. Pakistan has vowed retaliation and previously conducted strikes in Afghanistan, which Kabul denies.

title: 14 killed as Saudi Aramco helicopter crashes in Ras Tanura
content: Tragedy struck Saudi Arabia's eastern city of Ras Tanura on Sunday when a Saudi Aramco helicopter crashed, claiming the lives of fourteen individuals. The cause of the devastating incident remains under investigation by authorities. Saudi Aramco, a global leader in oil production, operates significant facilities in Ras Tanura, a vital hub for the kingdom's crude oil exports.

title: The price of paradise: What happens when offbeat travel destinations go mainstream
content: Once a cherished secret, India's offbeat travel destinations are rapidly becoming mainstream.  What began as a quiet escape for a few now faces overcrowding, mirroring global trends.  As infrastructure improves and social media fuels aspiration, the very charm of these places is threatened.  Travelers grapple with the desire for authentic experiences versus their role in accelerating discovery and its consequences.

title: Jeff Bezos vs Zohran Mamdani debate: Kevin O’Leary says Amazon founder should run cities
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos sparked a debate on government efficiency and taxation, suggesting New York City's services would falter under his company's operational model. Billionaire Kevin O'Leary echoed these sentiments, calling the city a 'disaster' and warning against taxing the wealthy. Former Mayor Bill de Blasio criticized Bezos' remarks as out of touch. Meanwhile, Bezos also dismissed fears of AI causing job losses, predicting a labor shortage instead.

title: Sooryavanshi misses out again as India hand debuts to Prince and Suryansh
content: India aims to level the T20I series against Ireland after a surprising loss in the opener. Captain Shreyas Iyer has introduced two debutants, Suryansh Shedge and Prince Yadav, to the playing XI.  The team is looking to bounce back and secure a victory in the second match of the series, with Shedge and Yadav eager to make their mark on the international stage.

title: ‘Poison or push?’: Siya Goyal, her ‘lover’ Chetan searched online for ways to kill Ketan, say cops
content: A chilling murder plot unfolded in Pune as a couple, Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary, meticulously planned realtor Ketan Agrawal's death. Online searches for murder methods and rehearsed attempts at Lohagad Fort reveal the premeditated nature of the crime. Driven by social pressures and a desire to prevent an arranged marriage, the duo executed their plan, making the fatal push appear accidental.

title: Michael Dell updates on Dell's Texas move days after Abbott's 'welcome home' message
content: Dell Technologies is officially moving its legal base from Delaware to Texas, with 97% of shareholders approving the change. CEO Michael Dell expressed pride in returning the company's legal home to the state where it was founded. This move aligns with Texas's growing appeal for major corporations, including Tesla, Coinbase, and Exxon Mobil, due to its favorable business climate and talent pool.

title: 'Age ko chhordiye': India-origin Ireland pacer Moondra makes Sooryavanshi admission
content: India-origin Ireland pacer Jai Moondra has backed teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, saying "age is just a number" and insisting the youngster has the potential to produce something special. Moondra's praise came after his dream debut for Ireland, where he claimed 2/25, including the wicket of Sanju Samson, helping Ireland register their first-ever international win over India.

title: Messi scripts history, breaks Brazil legend's FIFA WC record to become ...
content: The Argentina captain scored with a free-kick in the 80th minute at AT&T Stadium on Saturday (local time). It was his sixth World Cup goal from outside the box, taking him past Brazil great Rivelino, who had held the record with five.

title: Thar stunt, Fortuner chasing gazelle: Ladakh fines 4 in 1st crackdown on tourists
content: Ladakh's administration has taken unprecedented action against tourists for illegal off-roading in protected wildlife areas, including Pangong Lake. Four individuals faced hefty penalties totaling Rs 2 lakh and had their vehicles impounded for violating the Wildlife Protection Act. One offender was caught chasing a Tibetan gazelle within a sanctuary. This strict enforcement aims to safeguard Ladakh's fragile ecosystem and endangered wildlife, with authorities warning of continued stringent action against violators.

title: Land gifted for $10 as a park sold for $10 million after three decades
content: A Texas city sold 87 acres donated in 1999 for a $10 public park to a data center developer for $10 million. Residents, led by Pamela Griffin, are suing to honour the original deed, while Taylor officials say existing zoning permits the data center without resident approval. The project promises $30 million in tax revenue but raises concerns over noise, water use and property values.

title: 'They thought it was a picnic': Ex-cricketer tears into Team India after Ireland shock
content: Former India opener Sadagoppan Ramesh has strongly criticized Team India's complacent approach following their unexpected 34-run loss to Ireland. He believes the team underestimated their opponents, prioritizing the upcoming England series over the T20I opener. Ramesh highlighted Ireland's smart execution, particularly with short-pitched bowling on larger grounds, and pointed to India's sloppy fielding and overconfidence as key factors in the shocking defeat.

title: Bengal braces for UCC battle as BJP government prepares to table Bill in assembly
content: West Bengal's BJP government is set to introduce a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Bill, following similar moves in other BJP-ruled states. Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari confirmed the legislation will be presented Monday, aiming to replace religion-based personal laws with a common framework. The BJP asserts tribal communities will be exempt, while the Trinamool Congress criticizes the move as political polarization, demanding extensive consultation.

title: 'Visit Ram Temple, do penance to gain good sense': UP CM Yogi to Akhilesh amid donation row
content: Uttar Pradesh chief minister Yogi Adityanath has sharply criticized Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav over alleged donation embezzlement in Ayodhya, urging him to visit the Ram Temple. Adityanath accused the previous SP government of firing on Ram devotees and diverting funds to graveyards instead of temples. Meanwhile, Yadav questioned Adityanath's frequent visits, suggesting they were to oversee alleged thefts and criticized Ayodhya's development quality.

title: 'He'll be tired': Vaughan spots one weakness in Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's game
content: Former England captain Michael Vaughan lauded 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for revolutionizing T20 batting, pushing par scores to 230. However, Vaughan highlighted fielding endurance as Sooryavanshi's key challenge at the international level, suggesting England exploit this by having India field first.  Despite this, Sooryavanshi's impressive U19 World Cup and IPL performances showcase his potential.

title: Arunachal flash floods: Death toll rises to 3; IMD warns of over 200 mm of rainfall till Monday
content: Arunachal Pradesh grapples with rising flood death toll, now at three, after a missing man's body was recovered. Incessant rains triggered flash floods in Keyi Panyor district, displacing residents and damaging infrastructure. Rescue operations are ongoing, with NDRF personnel deployed to bolster efforts. Authorities have issued alerts for further heavy rainfall, warning of increased risks of landslides and waterlogging across multiple districts.

title: Venezuela quake: Race against time as rescuers battle rubble to find survivors; death toll tops 1400
content: Venezuela grapples with the aftermath of devastating twin earthquakes as the crucial 72-hour rescue window closes, dimming hopes for survivors. The death toll has tragically surpassed 1,430, with thousands injured and displaced. International aid and rescue teams are arriving, but damaged infrastructure, including hospitals, poses significant challenges, leaving an estimated 680,000 children in need of urgent assistance.

title: India's debt market not ready to fund next growth phase: Report
content: India's debt market requires urgent structural reforms to fund its ambitious economic growth, warns Deloitte. The report highlights that relying solely on bank deposits is no longer viable as household savings shift. To achieve its $7.3 trillion economy target by 2030, the market needs greater depth, liquidity, and integration to efficiently bridge the rising capital gap and support national economic aspirations.

title: Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw calls Bengaluru 'garbage city', sparks debate
content: Biocon founder Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw has voiced strong criticism regarding Bengaluru's urban development, lamenting the loss of its 'garden city' identity. She shared a stark visual comparison of current concrete-heavy infrastructure against a greener, more pedestrian-friendly alternative. Social media users echoed her concerns, attributing the city's decline to poor planning, corruption, and a lack of accountability from administrators and politicians.

title: Stokes angrily calls out Archer after pacer skips Bashir wicket celebration - Watch
content: England captain Ben Stokes was seen rallying Jofra Archer to join team celebrations during the third Test against New Zealand. After spinner Shoaib Bashir took a wicket, Archer initially stayed apart, seemingly frustrated by a dropped catch earlier. Stokes' decisive gesture emphasized team unity, drawing Archer back into the fold, a moment that resonated with fans praising his leadership.

title: Sonam Wangchuk begins indefinite hunger strike at Jantar Mantar in support of CJP protest
content: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk has commenced an indefinite hunger strike at Jantar Mantar, joining the Cockroach Janata Party's protest demanding Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. The demonstration, supported by students and farmer leaders, highlights alleged examination irregularities and calls for greater transparency and accountability in the education system. Concerns have been raised about potential house arrests of farmer leaders attempting to join the protest.

title: Iraqi security forces raid Baghdad Green Zone, arrest MPs in anti-corruption crackdown
content: Baghdad's high-security Green Zone saw a significant crackdown as Iraqi forces arrested politicians and officials in a sweeping anti-corruption drive. Judicial orders led to pre-dawn raids, targeting individuals implicated in financial corruption and potentially linked to smuggling and funding armed factions. This operation precedes Prime Minister Zaidi's US visit, signaling his commitment to reforms amidst ongoing investigations.

title: 3 firefighters killed battling wildfires on Colorado-Utah border, blazes spread across US West
content: Tragedy struck as three firefighters lost their lives battling blazes along the Colorado-Utah border, with two others injured. Extreme weather conditions are fueling widespread wildfires across the western US, including a massive blaze in southwest Utah that has scorched over 144 square miles. Officials are grappling with difficult terrain and dangerously low humidity, leading to a severe wildfire season and emergency declarations in multiple states.

title: Austrian GP 2026: Russell beats Ferrari duo for pole position, Verstappen crashes out
content: George Russell seized pole position for the Austrian Grand Prix after Max Verstappen crashed out in qualifying.  Russell's Mercedes outpaced Ferrari's Charles Leclerc and Lewis Hamilton, who will start second and third respectively.  Championship leader Kimi Antonelli qualified fourth, while Verstappen will start fifth.  The race promises a thrilling Mercedes-Ferrari showdown with Verstappen aiming to climb the field.

title: Crude, ceasefire and economic data: What's driving Dalal Street this week
content: Investors are bracing for a week dominated by Middle East geopolitical tensions, with crude oil prices and domestic economic data taking center stage. Key indicators like Industrial Production, PMI readings, and foreign exchange reserves will offer insights into India's economic health. Auto sales figures and monsoon progress also remain crucial. Any escalation in US-Iran relations could impact global growth and energy markets, while diplomatic progress might boost investor confidence.

title: Siya Goyal, her 'lover' Chetan held multiple rehearsals before murder; cops recreate crime scene
content: Two individuals, Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary, allegedly rehearsed Ketan Agrawal's murder multiple times before pushing him off a cliff at Lohagad Fort. Driven by social and family pressures to prevent an arranged marriage, the couple meticulously planned the crime, even attempting it earlier. Police recreated the scene and seized evidence, with a fast-track court now appointed for swift justice.

title: 'We've never met': Djokovic reveals years-long bond with Virat Kohli
content: Tennis icon Novak Djokovic revealed a unique bond with cricket star Virat Kohli, built on mutual respect despite never meeting. The two athletes have been exchanging messages, supporting each other's careers. Djokovic, aiming for his 25th Grand Slam, hopes to meet Kohli in India soon, a sentiment echoed by Kohli's ambition for a World Cup win in 2027. Their shared dedication to excellence transcends their respective sports.

title: Meet Aarya, Indian-origin toddler set to become the youngest person to visit all 63 American National Parks
content: An Indian-origin toddler, Aarya, is on a remarkable journey to become the youngest person to visit all 63 of America's national parks. At just 16 months old, she has already explored 34 parks with her parents, who were inspired by a park ranger. The family aims to foster a love for nature and conservation in Aarya, hoping to inspire others to explore the outdoors with their children.

title: Home loan mistakes that could cost you more, and how to avoid them
content: Securing a home loan is one of the critical steps in homeownership, yet many buyers stumble over common pitfalls. Avoiding these mistakes, such as not checking eligibility, ignoring credit scores, or borrowing beyond your means, can prevent financial stress and loan rejection. Thoroughly comparing lenders, understanding loan terms, and saving for a down payment are crucial for a smoother home-buying journey.

title: 'Oppn owes an apology to bravehearts': BJP accuses rivals of 'twisting' Rajnath's remarks
content: The BJP has strongly criticized the opposition for allegedly spreading "hostile propaganda" regarding India's counter-terror Operation Sindoor. Party leader Amit Malviya accused opposition leaders of misrepresenting defence minister Rajnath Singh's statement and demanded an apology for undermining the sacrifices of Indian soldiers. The BJP asserted Operation Sindoor was a major success, neutralizing over 100 terrorists and damaging Pakistani military infrastructure, while emphasizing the government's consistent honor for fallen heroes.

title: Isro moves step closer to more powerful LVM-3 with key engine test
content: Isro has successfully tested a new semi-cryogenic engine, achieving 175 tonnes of thrust. This crucial step towards a more powerful LVM-3 rocket is expected to significantly boost payload capacity for future launches. The engine's advanced turbopumps also performed exceptionally. This development is vital for India's growing ambitions in space exploration and commercial launches.

title: 'Cocktail 2' BO Day 10 [LIVE]: Crosses Rs 81 crore net milestone amid Welcome 3 clash
content: The Homi Adajania directorial 'Cocktail 2' is heading into its tenth day of release. The Shahid Kapoor, Rashmika Mandanna and Kriti Sanon starrer faces a direct box office battle with the newly released multi-starrer 'Welcome to the Jungle.' While Akshay Kumar's adventure-comedy captured a vast majority of single-screen layouts and prime-time multiplex slots over the weekend, 'Cocktail 2' leveraged glowing word-of-mouth to post a strong 6.3% second Saturday trajectory. Backed by solid international collection and steady urban multi-plex footfalls, the film is steadily veering towards the coveted Rs. 100 crore domestic club.

title: Why India's AMCA still needs a US engine, and so do many of the world's fighters
content: India's ambitious fifth-generation fighter jet program faces a significant hurdle as the cost of crucial US-made engines has reportedly tripled, jeopardizing timelines and budgets. This reliance on foreign propulsion systems, also impacting the Tejas Mk2, highlights a persistent challenge in developing world-class fighter jet engines domestically. While other nations also depend on foreign engines initially, India's pursuit of strategic autonomy hinges on mastering this complex technology.

title: Garg Aviation cadet pilot hurt while deboarding training plane with propeller running
content: A trainee pilot suffered severe back injuries after being hit by a running propeller while deboarding a trainer aircraft at Kanpur's Chakeri Airport. The Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) has launched a probe into the incident, highlighting a concerning practice of disembarking with engines running to expedite training. This raises questions about safety standards in some Indian flying training organizations, despite the booming aviation sector.

title: 'Meant to relieve pain': Rat poison capsules given at Moharram procession in Mumbai
content: A Pune man was apprehended in Byculla for distributing capsules containing zinc phosphide, a potent rat poison, to devotees during a Moharram procession. One devotee fell ill after consuming a capsule and required hospitalization. The accused claimed the capsules were for pain relief during mourning rituals. Police are investigating his motives, mental state, and how he obtained the large quantity of toxic substances.

title: Lionel Messi creates history, becomes first player ever to ...
content: Lionel Messi etched his name in World Cup history, becoming the first player to score in seven consecutive matches with a stunning free-kick against Jordan.  This remarkable goal, his 19th in the tournament, extended his all-time scoring record.  The Argentine captain, rested for the start, came off the bench to secure a 3-1 victory, showcasing his enduring impact on the global stage.

title: NAF gets Walmart Foundation grant to support 12,500 small farmers
content: Initiative to work with 10 FPOs across Rayalaseema and Coastal Andhra Pradesh to improve market access, promote climate-resilient farming and strengthen women’s livelihoods

title: Police raid houses of 8 arrested in Ayodhya donations case
content: Police teams, accompanied by local magistrates, carried out searches at the homes of the accused, including Lav Kush Mishra, Avinash Shukla and Ramashankar Yadav

title: Bhagyaraj’s last rites conducted with full state honours | Updates
content: He began his film career as an assistant director to veteran Tamil filmmaker Bharathiraja on the 1977 film 16 Vayathinile

title: India rejects Pakistan’s allegations on Karachi attack
content: “We have seen Pakistani reports making baseless allegations against India regarding the recent incident in Karachi. We categorically reject them,” the Ministry of External Affairs said

title: Ram Temple embezzlement case concerns assets of a juristic person and minor
content: Series of judicial decisions, right from the days of the Privy Council to Supreme Court’s judgments in the Sree Padmanabhaswamy Temple and the Ramjanmabhoomi Temple cases, hold the deity as a legal entity capable of holding property.

title: PM Modi owes an explanation to nation on Ayodhya Ram Temple donation embezzlement case: Pinarayi Vijayan
content: Those who exploited people’s faith for political gains now face allegations of organised financial fraud, says Kerala Leader of the Opposition in a Facebook post

title: Kerala LSG Minister K.M. Shaji kicks up a storm with his support for display of PMAY logo in front of houses
content: Minister’s stand is in sharp contrast to previous LDF government’s, which had opposed the same citing the dignity of the beneficiaries of the housing scheme

title: Fishing hamlets of Kanniyakumari’s Thoothoor zone where football fuels dreams
content: The Thoothoor zone comprises Erayumanthurai, Poothurai, Thoothoor, Chinnathurai, Eraviputhenthurai, Vallavilai, Marthandanthurai and Neerodi, the last coastal village of the Tamil Nadu bordering Kerala.

title: MEA issues advisory for citizens undertaking Mansarovar Yatra through private tour operators
content: The Mansarovar Yatra resumed last year after a gap of nearly five years as part of efforts to normalise the relations between India and China

title: Health Ministry to launch revised Anaemia Mukt Bharat guidelines
content: Expanded Anaemia Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan framework introduces a new beneficiary group, dietary interventions and digital tracking to strengthen anaemia control

title: Gold worth ₹10 lakh missing from Karipur police station in Malappuram; former SHO under scanner
content: An inquiry by a team led by  DySP, Kondotty, found the alleged involvement of the former SHO, who is under suspension in another disciplinary case

title: Seven accused nabbed by Bengaluru CCB planned revenge killing of history-sheeter
content: The plot, orchestrated by Sabrina Taj and her brother-in-law Shakeel Shabbir, aimed to eliminate a rival gang member in retaliation for a past murder

title: Body of missing girl recovered after her parents, brother found drowned in Piravom
content: The body of her father Narayanan was recovered from the waters near Mulakkulam-Paittakuzhi bridge on June 27, a day after his wife Viji, a 40-year-old visually-impaired woman, and their two-year-old son were found dead in the river.

title: Karnataka Chief Minister lays foundation stone for Hebbal short tunnel project
content: The development comes even as civic groups and activists have criticised the project, calling it an unnecessary expenditure

title: BDA to execute short tunnel at Hebbal under EPC mode in Bengaluru
content: Officials said that the successful bidder has begun an alignment survey to determine the exact route and engineering configuration of the tunnel, including its entry and exit points

title: Woman booked for attacking hospital staff and creating nuisance at LB Nagar private hospital
content: The accused, identified as K. Lalitha, was taken to Shreshta Orange Hospital after complaining of a stomach ache. According to the police, she turned violent after doctors examined her and told her that her condition was not serious

title: Mamata loyalists file police complaint against ‘misuse’ of party name, symbol by rebels
content: Trinamool Congress MP Dola Sen has filed complaint alleging that a section of leaders was creating confusion among party workers, and damaging the reputation and credibility of the party.

title: Residents of actor-director Bhagyaraj’s native Vellankovil village hold candlelight procession in his memory
content: The procession was organised by the villagers and Bhagyaraj’s childhood friends to mourn the death of the filmmaker

title: Woman’s death confirmed as murder; hunt on for live-in partner
content: The body of the victim, identified as Akshata Indaragi (30) from Ilkal in Bagalkote district, was found in a decomposed State on June 17 at a rented house near Patalamma Temple in Jigani, Anekal taluk

title: New EPS rules leave out clause on higher pension
content: EPFO board describes as obsolete the clause that provided a maximum of one year to  employers and employees to exercise the higher pension option by making contributions above the wage cap of ₹15,000 every month

title: Parliamentary panel suggests a raise to EPF pension, says ₹1,000 inadequate to meet basic needs
content: This assumes significance in view of pensioners' demand to raise the pension to ₹7,500 per month, as ₹1,000 is not sufficient to make both ends meet

title: See children as wealth, not a burden: Chandrababu Naidu at Pulse Polio launch
content: Launching the three-day drive to cover 49.2 lakh children under five, the Chief Minister said the government was offering incentives to families to have two or more children

title: Despite grave concerns raised in the White Paper, Kerala Budget silent on bringing down committed expenditure
content: Committed expenditure is projected to exceed ₹1.22 lakh crore in 2026-27, with salaries, pensions and interest payments consuming nearly three-fourths of Kerala’s revenues

title: Kerala’s fiscal status report: Its politics and economics
content: The report places considerable emphasis on Kerala’s outstanding liabilities. But the rising debt was largely a product of the Covid-19 pandemic, when the State made full use of the higher borrowing limit allowed by the Union

title: India’s vision is to make Indian Ocean an Ocean of Opportunity: PM Modi in Seychelles
content: Seychelles confers the title of ‘Guardian of the Blue Horizon’ on PM Modi; Delegation-level talks held by PM Modi with Seychelles President Herminie to deepen bilateral ties

title: Nadigar Sangam elections case: Madras High Court to pass orders on July 1
content: Justice A.D. Maria Clete has reserved orders on a plea to reject a suit filed against the continuance of office-bearers, elected in 2022, till 2028

title: From seas to skies, India is becoming increasingly self-reliant: PM Modi
content: In his monthly radio broadcast 'Mann Ki Baat', PM Modi noted that in June itself, the country achieved a major success in the aviation sector

title: Bhogapuram airport’s user fee higher than Delhi, Hyderabad; travellers seek a cut
content: AERA has cleared a ₹835 domestic and ₹1,255 international departure fee at Bhogapuram, with charges even on arriving passengers, drawing calls for a rollback

title: Woman booked for assaulting a sanitation worker over objection to roadside garbage dumping
content: According to Jagadgirigutta Inspector B. Venkatesham, the accused, whose identity is yet to be established, arrived on a motorcycle with a bag of garbage and dumped it on the roadside

title: Telangana rolls out Pulse Polio campaign with target to vaccinate over 40 lakh children
content: Though there have been no polio cases for over a decade now, we still need to take precautions to maintain it that way, says Health Minister C. Damodar Raja Narasimha

title: Sonam Wangchuk joins CJP protest, begins hunger strike at Jantar Mantar
content: Hundreds of protesters, mostly youth and students, gathered at Jantar Mantar as Mr. Wangchuk began his fast. Several farmer leaders were also present at the protest site. The protest began with a two-minute silence

title: What does the Centre’s Vision Document on Drug Control 2026-2029 propose?
content: The three-year road map on drug control seeks to dismantle narcotics networks through intelligence-led enforcement, technology, preventive detention, rehabilitation, and coordinated action against inter-State and transnational drug cartels

title: Reliance to donate Tirumala 25 electric buses worth ₹27.5 crore; Anant Ambani donates hair
content: The Reliance Industries executive director said the company would also pay the 50 drivers and help set up EV charging stations to run the buses

title: YSRCP insists on transparency in SIR process
content: It urges CEO to share constituency-wise and booth-wise voter lists in a digital, searchable format, along with complete BLO details

title: YSRCP accuses coalition govt. of electoral malpractices in Pulivendula and Ontimitta ZPTC byelections
content: The party leaders allege rampant voter intimidation, cash distribution, and administrative misuse, urging the State Election Commission to intervene for free and fair polls

title: All Sikh MLAs, Ministers to appear before Akal Takht on June 29: Mann
content: Akal Takht Jathedar Giani Kuldip Singh Gargajj, on June 15, had summoned all Sikh MLAs, regardless of party affiliation, and the Sikh ministers before the Akal Takht on June 29 regarding the anti-sacrilege law

title: NDA government interfering in SIR, alleges YSRCP’s Kakani Govardhan Reddy
content: The YSRCP leader objected to district Minister P. Narayana meeting booth level officers, and said the SIR should not be used to remove voters of any party

title: Edappadi Palaniswami flags irregularities in recent recruitment of teachers in Tamil Nadu; calls for enquiry
content: He further called for an independent enquiry and further insisted that recruitments should be suspended till the completion of the enquiry.

title: Directorate of Cashew Research gets Innovation and Technology Advancement Award by BCIC
content: The award was presented during the first edition of the Agro-Food Processing Awards 2026, held in Bengaluru recently. The award recognises the DCR’s contributions towards developing innovative technologies that have the potential for transforming India’s cashew sector

title: National drug survey to study indigenous substance use patterns this year
content: Nearly 20 lakh people to be sampled for this survey on substance use and related disorders across country, report expected to be published by 2027

title: Pulse Polio drive from June 28; drops for children under five at 1,937 centres in Tirupati
content: Drops will be given at 1,937 centres in the district on Sunday, with door-to-door visits by staff on Monday and Tuesday to reach children missed at the centres

title: Tamil Nadu likely to see scattered rain till June 30
content: The southwest monsoon has been in its normal phase in the State. Most districts have received their normal share or surplus rainfall so far this June.

title: Tamil Nadu government cancels DFR tender for 12 municipal corporations, cites ‘confusion’ over waste management privatisation
content: To clear the confusion, the State has decided to review the existing system, identify gaps, bring further improvements where necessary, and take the decision in due course, an official said

title: ‘Blatant loot’: Congress on MoS Agriculture getting subsidy from own ministry
content: In yet another blow to the 'Chanda-Chor' RSS-BJP's claims of zero tolerance for corruption, Union Minister of State for Agriculture Bhagirath Choudhary approved a ₹99.03 lakh subsidy for his own project, alleges Congress leader Pawan Khera

title: Chandrababu Naidu, Pawan Kalyan to launch VB-G RAM G in Tirupati district on July 2
content: The launch at Mukkavaripalle, the constituency’s first big event since its merger with Tirupati, will be attended by Union Ministers Shivraj Singh Chauhan and Pemmasani Chandrasekhar

title: Pune fort murder | The scorpion’s sting
content: Ketan Agarwal was engaged to Siya Goyal in February and a lavish wedding was planned for November. On June 18, Ketan was found dead at the bottom of a 400-foot gorge at Lohagad Fort in Pune. What followed was a police investigation that peeled back, layer by layer, a plot allegedly months in the making

title: Man arrested for allegedly cheating private schools across Tamil Nadu of over ₹100 crore
content: The police said the accused allegedly operated the unregistered organisation under the name of ‘Tamil Nadu Private Schools Association’ and claimed to have political and bureaucratic influence

title: YSRCP’s fight is for power and ours is for public service, says A.P. Minister Kandula Durgesh
content: Tourism Minister accuses YSRCP of consistently opposing the capital city and attempting to provoke people through irresponsible statements

title: 'Fake News': Trump on book that says he leaves leftover snack, silverware on bedroom floor

title: Seychelles Honours PM Modi With 'Guardian Of The Blue Horizon' Title
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was conferred with the honorary 'Guardian of the Blue Horizon' title by Seychelles for his steadfast commitment to environmental conservation and advancing the aspirations of the Small Island Developing States.
